sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 16.77 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.16)

statement.latex: It is known that in every Noetherian group the nilpotent radical coincides with the collection of all Engel elements (R.\,Baer, Math. Ann., 133 (1957), 256--270; B.\,I.\,Plotkin, Izv. Vys {s. U ebn. Zaved. Mat.}, { 1958}, no.\,1(2), 130--135 (Russian)). It would be nice to find a similar characterization of the solvable radical of a finite group. More precisely, let$u=u(x,y)$be a sequence of words...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
